subtitle: A 7-Day Devotional
description: The blood of Christ is not symbolic sentiment — it is the foundation of everything. Seven days sitting with what it means: forgiveness, cleansing, justification, reconciliation, freedom, bold access, and new life.

days.title: The Foundation: What the Blood of Christ Means
days.subtitle: Day 1
days.theme: Without blood, there is no forgiveness
days.reading: From the earliest pages of Scripture, God establishes a sobering truth: sin carries a cost. Under the Old Covenant, sacrifices temporarily covered sin. But the blood of Christ doesn't just cover — it removes.
days.forBeliever: The blood of Jesus is not symbolic sentiment — it is the legal basis of your forgiveness. Your standing with God is not built on performance, but on a finished sacrifice.
days.forUnbeliever: Forgiveness is not earned through effort or morality. The invitation is simple but weighty: accept what has already been paid.

reflection.prompt: Do you see forgiveness as something you maintain, or something Christ secured?

choices.label: I struggle to feel truly forgiven
choices.deepQuestion: What would it mean to trust the blood of Christ over your feelings? What would living from that certainty change in your daily life?

choices.label: I know it in my head but not my heart
choices.deepQuestion: What specific area of your life are you still performing for God rather than resting in what He has already done?

choices.label: I'm beginning to rest in it
choices.deepQuestion: How does living from a secured forgiveness change the way you relate to others — those who have hurt you, or those still searching?

days.abide: Lord, help me understand the weight and sufficiency of Your sacrifice.

days.title: Cleansed from Sin
days.subtitle: Day 2
days.reading: The blood of Christ doesn't partially clean — it purifies entirely. There is no category of sin it cannot cover.
days.forBeliever: When guilt lingers, it's often because you trust your feelings more than the blood. Cleansing is not progressive — it is decisive.
days.forUnbeliever: No past is too dark. The same blood that saves saints was shed for sinners.

reflection.prompt: Are you holding onto guilt that God has already removed?

choices.label: Yes — there's something I can't let go of
choices.deepQuestion: If God declares you clean through the blood of Christ, what would it look like to agree with Him — not just in word, but in how you walk into tomorrow?

choices.label: Guilt creeps back in even after I confess
choices.deepQuestion: What is the difference between conviction that leads you to God and condemnation that keeps you from Him? Which one are you listening to?

choices.label: I'm learning to walk in freedom
choices.deepQuestion: Who around you is still carrying guilt they haven't released? How might your freedom become a gift to them?

days.abide: Jesus, teach me to live as someone truly cleansed.

days.title: Justified and Made Righteous
days.subtitle: Day 3
days.reading: Justification is a legal declaration: not guilty. But more than that, Christ's righteousness is credited to you.
days.forBeliever: You are not on probation with God. The blood has already secured your right standing.
days.forUnbeliever: You don't need to fix yourself first. Justification happens at the moment of faith, not after self-improvement.

reflection.prompt: Do you relate to God as a judge waiting to condemn, or as one who has already justified you?

choices.label: Mostly as a judge — I'm waiting for Him to find fault
choices.deepQuestion: What would change in your prayer life and your daily walk if you truly believed God looks at you and sees Christ's righteousness — not your record?

choices.label: Somewhere in between
choices.deepQuestion: What has shaped your picture of God — Scripture or something else? What would it look like to let Romans 5:9 reframe how you see Him?

choices.label: I'm learning to see Him as the one who already justified me
choices.deepQuestion: How does knowing you are not on probation free you to serve God from love rather than fear? What does that look like today?

days.abide: Father, anchor me in the truth of my righteousness in Christ.

days.title: Reconciled to God
days.subtitle: Day 4
days.reading: Sin separates; the blood restores relationship. Reconciliation means hostility is replaced with peace.
days.forBeliever: God is not distant or reluctant. The blood has already brought you near.
days.forUnbeliever: God is not your enemy. Through Christ, He has made the first move toward peace.

reflection.prompt: Do you approach God freely, or with hesitation?

choices.label: With hesitation — I'm not sure He wants to hear from me
choices.deepQuestion: Colossians 1:20 says peace has already been made through His blood. What is keeping you from receiving what God has already extended to you?

choices.label: It depends on how I've been living
choices.deepQuestion: If your access to God depended on your behavior, you could never be sure. But if it depends on the blood — what changes? How do you come to Him now?

choices.label: Freely — I know He has brought me near
choices.deepQuestion: Who in your life is living as if God is far or against them? How might you reflect the reconciling love of Christ toward that person?

days.abide: Lord, help me live in the nearness You purchased.

days.title: Freedom from the Power of Sin
days.subtitle: Day 5
days.reading: The blood of Christ doesn't just forgive sin — it breaks its authority.
days.forBeliever: You are no longer a slave to sin. Patterns can be broken, not by willpower alone, but by the power of what Christ has done.
days.forUnbeliever: Freedom is not found in self-control alone. True liberation begins with surrender to Christ.

reflection.prompt: Where are you still living as if you are bound?

choices.label: There's a pattern I keep returning to
choices.deepQuestion: Revelation 1:5 says He has released us from our sins by His blood — past tense, finished. What would it mean to stop fighting in your own strength and start standing in what is already true?

choices.label: I know I'm free but I don't feel it
choices.deepQuestion: Freedom in Christ is a reality before it is a feeling. What would it look like to act on what is true — even before you feel it — and what would that require of you today?

choices.label: I'm walking in greater freedom than before
choices.deepQuestion: What did that freedom cost you — and what did it cost Christ? How does gratitude for that keep you walking forward?

days.abide: Jesus, teach me to walk in the freedom Your blood provides.

days.title: Bold Access to God
days.subtitle: Day 6
days.reading: Before Christ, access to God was restricted. Now, because of His blood, believers can come boldly.
days.forBeliever: Prayer is not a privilege you earn — it's access you've been given.
days.forUnbeliever: You don't need a mediator other than Christ. You can come directly to God through Him.

reflection.prompt: Do you pray with confidence or hesitation?

choices.label: With hesitation — I feel unworthy to come
choices.deepQuestion: Hebrews 10:19 says you have confidence to enter by the blood of Jesus — not by your worthiness. What would it look like to take God at His word and come boldly today?

choices.label: It depends on the day or how I've been living
choices.deepQuestion: Your access to God is not based on your performance — it's based on a finished sacrifice. What would consistent, confident prayer look like if you lived from that reality?

choices.label: I'm learning to pray with more boldness
choices.deepQuestion: What has changed in your prayer life as you've grown in confidence? What would you say to someone who still feels too small to come to God?

days.abide: Father, help me approach You with boldness and trust.

days.title: A New Covenant and New Life
days.subtitle: Day 7
days.reading: The blood of Christ established a new covenant — based not on human faithfulness, but on His.
days.forBeliever: Your relationship with God is sustained by grace, not law. You live from acceptance, not toward it.
days.forUnbeliever: This new covenant is an open invitation: new life, new identity, and a restored relationship with God.

reflection.prompt: Are you living like someone under grace or still striving under law?

choices.label: Still striving — I feel like I have to earn my place
choices.deepQuestion: The new covenant is based on His faithfulness, not yours. What would it feel like to stop earning and start receiving? What is the first thing that would change?

choices.label: Somewhere in between — grace in my head, law in my heart
choices.deepQuestion: What part of you still believes that God's acceptance of you depends on your performance? How does Luke 22:20 challenge that belief?

choices.label: I'm resting more and more in grace
choices.deepQuestion: As you close this seven-day journey, what is the one truth about the blood of Christ that has settled most deeply in you? How will you carry it forward?

days.abide: Lord, teach me to live fully in the new life You've given.
